steps.title: Pin down the goal
steps.content: The student wants to describe the rocking chair to an audience that does not know Sam Maloof. So the sentence must (1) identify Maloof as the maker and (2) describe the chair itself.

steps.title: Look for a choice that does both
steps.content: The sentence that names Maloof and describes the chair's sleek, contoured armrests and seat, walnut material, and placement in Boston's Museum of Fine Arts satisfies the note-taking goal.

steps.title: Eliminate per choice
steps.content:
• Choice A — With its sleek, contoured armrests and seat, the walnut rocking chair in Boston's Museum of Fine Arts is just one piece of furniture created by American woodworker Sam Maloof.: identifies Maloof for the unfamiliar audience and describes the chair using relevant notes. ✓
  • Choice B — Sam Maloof was born in 1916 and died in 2009, and during his life, he made a chair that you can see if you visit the Museum of Fine Arts in Boston.: gives biographical dates but no description of the chair (overreach on biography, misread of the goal). ✗
  • Choice C — Furniture designer Sam Maloof was a recipient of one of the John D. and Catherine T. MacArthur Foundation's "genius grants.": focuses on an award and never describes the chair at all (misread of the goal). ✗
  • Choice D — The rocking chair is made from walnut, and it has been shaped such that its armrests and seat are sleek and contoured.: describes the chair but never names Maloof, so the unfamiliar audience is not introduced to him (misread of the audience constraint). ✗
A is correct.
